mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and support risk asset valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals accelerating labor market recovery, lifting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising retail sales and falling unemployment indicate robust consumer demand coincident with income growth, producing stronger positive S&P 500 returns than either signal in isolation over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and UNRATE change achieves a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During high-unemployment regimes, month-over-month declines in payroll employment trigger negative S&P 500 returns because job losses intensify fears of a demand collapse that corporate earnings cannot withstand.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when PAYEMS declines while UNRATE exceeds 8%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and improving capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 returns as operating leverage lifts profit margins.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of triggered instances during any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts exceeding one hundred thousand units over a three-month period signals a contraction in residential construction and downstream consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales reflect a balanced expansion where supply meets demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to produce positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment grows by more than two hundred thousand over three months while the federal funds rate stays below fifty basis points, the S&P 500 delivers positive quarterly returns as markets price a Goldilocks recovery.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not positive in more than 60% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.
